description: English with a soft West African accent (Guinean Fulani under decades of American English). Female, elderly, early 70s. Worn but clear. Persona: dignified, wry survivor. Emotion: gentle, weary, wryly honest. Quiet, breath-short timbre with soft consonants and a gentle, even rhythm; speaks in plain complete sentences, growing calmer under strain, a faint dry humor beneath the fatigue.
text: [sighs] I should have come before. I kept thinking what it would run me... old foolishness. Fifty years afraid of the cost, and then it's gone, and you're still afraid. [warm] But you're warm here. That's something.
